title: ZPA Application Segment Health
spl:
index=zt sourcetype="zscaler:zpa" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|down") OR latency_ms>2000
| stats count by app_segment, connector_group, error_code
| sort 30 -count
description: Tracks connector health, app segment reachability, and error rates for ZPA-published apps.
value: Tracks connector health, app segment reachability, and error rates for ZPA-published apps.
implementation: Normalize `app_segment` and latency fields from your ZPA TA. Alert on connector group with >5% error rate vs prior week.
visualization: Table (unhealthy segments), Line chart (error rate), Single value (segments in alert).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Zscaler TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zscaler:zpa`, connector telemetry.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize `app_segment` and latency fields from your ZPA TA. Alert on connector group with >5% error rate vs prior week.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="zscaler:zpa" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|down") OR latency_ms>2000
| stats count by app_segment, connector_group, error_code
| sort 30 -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**ZPA Application Segment Health** — Tracks connector health, app segment reachability, and error rates for ZPA-published apps.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:zpa`, connector telemetry. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Zscaler TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: zt; **sourcetype**: zscaler:zpa. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=zt, sourcetype="zscaler:zpa", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|down") OR latency_ms>2000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_segment, connector_group, error_code** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Zscaler ZPA admin for the same applications, connectors, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unhealthy segments), Line chart (error rate), Single value (segments in alert).
